text: In this visualization, we'll compare three popular optimization algorithms used in training neural networks: SGD, Adam, and RMSProp.
The elliptical contours represent a loss surface, with warmer colors indicating higher loss. The arrows show the gradient direction pointing toward the minimum at the center.
All three optimizers start from the same position. Watch as they take different paths to reach the minimum.
The red path represents SGD, or Stochastic Gradient Descent. Notice how it oscillates significantly and takes many steps to converge.
The blue path shows Adam, which uses adaptive learning rates and momentum. It converges smoothly and fastest among the three.
The green path represents RMSProp, which adapts the learning rate per parameter. It converges faster than SGD but maintains stability.
Key observations: SGD is simple but slow with more oscillation. Adam offers the fastest, smoothest convergence. RMSProp provides a balanced approach with adaptive step sizes.
Understanding these differences helps you choose the right optimizer for your neural network training.
